title: PERSIST
description: Removes the expiration time of a key.

returns.description: 1 if the timeout was removed, 0 if key does not exist or does not have an associated timeout
1 if the timeout was removed, 0 if key does not exist or does not have an associated timeout
Boolean integer-reply specifically: true if the timeout was removed. false if key does not exist or does not have an associated timeout.
Boolean integer-reply specifically: true if the timeout was removed. false if key does not exist or does not have an associated timeout.
Boolean integer-reply specifically: true if the timeout was removed. false if key does not exist or does not have an associated timeout.

params.description: The flags to use for this operation.
params.name: flags

returns.description: true if the timeout was removed. false if key does not exist or does not have an associated timeout.

params.description: The flags to use for this operation.
params.name: flags

returns.description: true if the timeout was removed. false if key does not exist or does not have an associated timeout.
whether the timeout was removed or not

content:
Remove the existing timeout on `key`, turning the key from _volatile_ (a key with an expire set) to _persistent_ (a key that will never expire as no timeout is associated). ## Examples {{% redis-cli %}} SET mykey "Hello" EXPIRE mykey 10 TTL mykey PERSIST mykey TTL mykey {{% /redis-cli %}} ## Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility | Redis
Software | Redis
Cloud | Notes | |:----------------------|:-----------------|:------| | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | | ## Return information {{< multitabs id="persist-return-info" tab1="RESP2" tab2="RESP3" >}} One of the following: * [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): `0` if _key_ does not exist or does not have an associated timeout. * [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): `1` if the timeout has been removed. -tab-sep- One of the following: * [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): `0` if _key_ does not exist or does not have an associated timeout. * [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): `1` if the timeout has been removed. {{< /multitabs >}}
example:
SET mykey "Hello"
EXPIRE mykey 10
TTL mykey
PERSIST mykey
TTL mykey
intro:
Remove the existing timeout on `key`, turning the key from _volatile_ (a key
with an expire set) to _persistent_ (a key that will never expire as no timeout
is associated).

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
Remove the existing timeout on `key`, turning the key from _volatile_ (a key
with an expire set) to _persistent_ (a key that will never expire as no timeout
is associated).

sections.title: Examples
sections.content:
{{% redis-cli %}}
SET mykey "Hello"
EXPIRE mykey 10
TTL mykey
PERSIST mykey
TTL mykey
{{% /redis-cli %}}

sections.title: Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility
sections.content:
| Redis
Software | Redis
Cloud | Notes | |:----------------------|:-----------------|:------| | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | |

sections.title: Return information
sections.content:
{{< multitabs id="persist-return-info" 
    tab1="RESP2" 
    tab2="RESP3" >}}

One of the following:
* [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): `0` if _key_ does not exist or does not have an associated timeout.
* [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): `1` if the timeout has been removed.

-tab-sep-

One of the following:
* [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): `0` if _key_ does not exist or does not have an associated timeout.
* [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): `1` if the timeout has been removed.

{{< /multitabs >}}
